info.title: TransitOps API
info.description: Fleet operations platform — manage vehicles, drivers, trips, maintenance, fuel, expenses, and reports.

post.description: Create a new user account. No authentication required.
Authenticate with email and password. Returns a JWT access token.

get.description: Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user.
Returns all vehicles. Supports optional filters by type, status, and region.

post.description: Register a new vehicle. Requires fleet_manager role. Rule 1: registration_number must be unique.

get.description: Returns a single vehicle by its UUID.

put.description: Update vehicle fields. Requires fleet_manager role. Rule 1: registration_number uniqueness is re-checked.

delete.description: Delete a vehicle by ID. Requires fleet_manager role.

post.description: Register a new driver. Requires fleet_manager role. License number must be unique.

get.description: Returns a single driver by UUID.

put.description: Update driver fields. fleet_manager: all fields. safety_officer: compliance fields only (license_*, safety_score, status).

delete.description: Delete a driver by ID. Requires fleet_manager role.

get.description: Returns all trips. Drivers only see their own assigned trips.

post.description: Create a trip in Draft status. Validates Rules 2-5 at creation. Requires fleet_manager or driver role.
Transition Draft → Dispatched. Re-validates Rules 2, 3, 4 (race-condition guard). Rule 6: sets vehicle + driver to On Trip.
Transition Dispatched → Completed. Rule 7: restores vehicle + driver to Available. Design decision #1: auto-creates FuelLog. Design decision #2: updates Vehicle.odometer_km.

Cancel a Draft or Dispatched trip. Design decision #3: Draft cancellation has no side effects. Dispatched cancellation restores vehicle + driver to Available (Rule 8). Completed/Cancelled trips cannot be cancelled (409).

409.description: Trip cannot be cancelled from current status

post.description: Create a new maintenance record for a vehicle. Rule 9: automatically sets the vehicle status to 'In Shop'.
Close an open maintenance record. Rule 10: restores vehicle to 'Available' unless 'Retired'.

get.description: Returns all fuel logs. Optional filter by vehicle_id.

schema.title: Response List Fuel Logs Fuel Expense Fuel Get

post.description: Record a fuel log entry for a vehicle.

get.description: Returns all expenses. Optional filter by vehicle_id.

schema.title: Response List Expenses Fuel Expense Expenses Get

post.description: Record an expense entry for a vehicle.

get.summary: Get total operational cost for a vehicle
get.description: Computes total operational cost = Fuel + Maintenance + Expenses for a vehicle.
Returns fleet KPIs. Supports optional filters by vehicle type, status, and region (applied to vehicle-related KPIs).
Distance / Fuel per vehicle. Fuel data sourced from FuelLog (canonical).

schema.title: Response Fuel Efficiency Report Reports Fuel Efficiency Get

get.description: Vehicles On Trip / total active vehicles.

schema.title: Response Operational Cost Report Reports Operational Cost Get
Response Vehicle Roi Report Reports Vehicle Roi Get

description.title: Description
examples: Completed oil change and filter replacement

final_odometer_km.title: Final Odometer Km
final_odometer_km.description: If provided, sets vehicle odometer to this value. Otherwise, odometer is incremented by actual_distance_km.

TripComplete.title: TripComplete
TripComplete.description: Payload for completing a trip — design decisions #1 and #2.
